channel.title: Critical-Process on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Critical-Process on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] BSOD CRITICAL_PROCESS_DIED Windows 11/10 — Fixed
item.description:
[Solution] BSOD CRITICAL_PROCESS_DIED Windows 11/10 — Fixed

CRITICAL_PROCESS_DIED is a critical Blue Screen of Death error with stop code 0x000000EF. It occurs when a critical system process — such as csrss.exe, wininit.exe, smss.exe, or winlogon.exe — terminates unexpectedly. These processes are essential for Windows operation, and when they crash, Windows cannot continue running.

This BSOD affects both Windows 10 and 11 and can occur during boot (creating a recovery loop), during normal use, or after a Windows Update or driver installation.

item.title: [Solution] Error 0x000000ef Windows 11/10 — CRITICAL_PROCESS_DIED Fix
item.description:
[Solution] Error 0x000000ef Windows 11/10 — CRITICAL_PROCESS_DIED Fix

Error 0x000000ef is a BSOD STOP error with the bug check CRITICAL_PROCESS_DIED. It occurs when a critical system process terminates unexpectedly, and Windows cannot recover. The process most commonly involved is csrss.exe, wininit.exe, or smss.exe.

This error affects both Windows 10 and 11 and can occur during boot, during use, or in a recurring loop that prevents normal operation.

Description

The full error message typically reads:
